1: export function chargeCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
2:   if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }
3:   gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)
4:   return { ok: true }
5: }
6: 
7: export function refundCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
8:   gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)
9:   return { ok: true }

display.text: [![GitHub Workflow Status](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/honojs/hono/ci.yml?branch=main)](https://github.com/honojs/hono/actions) [![GitHub](https://img.shields.io/github/license/honojs/hono)](https://github.com/honojs/hono/blob/main/LICENSE) [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/hono)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/hono) [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/dm/hono)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/hono) [![JSR](https://jsr.io/badges/@hono/hono)](https://jsr.io/@hono/hono) [![Bundle Size](https://img.shields.io/bundlephobia/min/hono)](https://bundlephobia.com/result?p=hono) [![Bundle Size](https://img.shields.io/bundlephobia/minzip/hono)](https://bundlephobia.com/result?p=hono) [![GitHub commit activity](https://img.shields.io/github/commit-activity/m/honojs/hono)](https://github.com/honojs/hono/pulse) [![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/honojs/hono)](https://github.com/honojs/hono/commits/main) [![codecov](https://codecov.io/github/honojs/hono/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/github/honojs/hono) [![Discord badge](https://img.shields.io/discord/1011308539819597844?label=Discord&logo=Discord)](https://discord.gg/KMh2eNSdxV) [![Ask DeepWiki](https://deepwiki.com/badge.svg)](https://deepwiki.com/honojs/hono) Hono - _**means flame🔥 in Japanese**_ - is a small, simple, and ultrafast web framework built on Web Standards. It works on any JavaScript runtime: Cloudflare Workers, Fastly Compute, Deno, Bun, Vercel, AWS Lambda, Lambda@Edge, and Node.js. Fast, but not only fast. ```ts import { Hono } from 'hono' const app = new Hono() app.get('/', (c) => c.text('Hono!')) export default app ``` ## Quick Start ```bash npm create hono@latest ``` ## Features - **Ultrafast** 🚀 - The router `RegExpRouter` is really fast. Not using linear loops. Fast. - **Lightweight** 🪶 - The `hono/tiny` preset is under 12kB. Hono has zero dependencies and uses only the Web Standard API. - **Multi-runtime** 🌍 - Works on Cloudflare Workers, Fastly Compute, Deno, Bun, AWS Lambda, Lambda@Edge, or Node.js. The same code runs on all platforms. - **Batteries Included** 🔋 - Hono has built-in middleware, custom middleware, and third-party middleware. Batteries included. - **Delightful DX** 😃 - Super clean APIs. First-class TypeScript support. Now, we've got "Types". ## Documentation The documentation is available on [hono.dev](https://hono.dev). ## Migration The migration guide is available on [docs/MIGRATION.md](docs/MIGRATION.md). ## Communication [X](https://x.com/honojs) and [Discord channel](https://discord.gg/KMh2eNSdxV) are available. ## Contributing Contributions Welcome! You can contribute in the following ways. - Create an Issue - Propose a new feature. Report a bug. - Pull Request - Fix a bug or typo. Refactor the code. - Create third-party middleware - See instructions below. - Share - Share your thoughts on the Blog, X, and others. - Make your application - Please try to use Hono. For more details, see [docs/CONTRIBUTING.md](docs/CONTRIBUTING.md). ## Contributors Thanks to [all contributors](https://github.com/honojs/hono/graphs/contributors)! ## Authors Yusuke Wada _RegExpRouter_, _SmartRouter_, _LinearRouter_, and _PatternRouter_ are created by Taku Amano ## License Distributed under the MIT License. See [LICENSE](LICENSE) for more information.

state.title: Check for existing artifact files

input.content:
# Code Review Diagnosis

## File: bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs

## Findings

### chargeCustomer: Missing amount validation
- **Location:** L1-L4
- **Issue:** The `amount` parameter is never validated. Type checking, range validation, and positive value checks are missing.
- **Risk:** Invalid or negative amounts can be charged, leading to security and financial issues.
- **Fix:** Add amount type and value validation before calling gateway.charge()

### chargeCustomer: Missing gateway error handling
- **Location:** L3
- **Issue:** `gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)` has no error handling. If the gateway throws or fails, the function will unhandledly crash.
- **Risk:** Runtime crashes, unhandled exceptions, poor developer experience. Function should return proper error state on failure.
- **Fix:** Wrap in try-catch, return `{ ok: false, error: <error-from-gateway> }` on failure

### refundCustomer: Missing customer validation
- **Location:** L7-L9
- **Issue:** No validation that customer exists or has valid ID before attempting refund.
- **Risk:** Refund operation will fail when customer is missing or invalid.
- **Fix:** Check customer.id exists, similar to chargeCustomer.

### refundCustomer: Missing amount validation
- **Location:** L8
- **Issue:** The `amount` parameter is never validated.
- **Risk:** Invalid or negative amounts can be refunded.
- **Fix:** Add amount type and value validation before calling gateway.refund()

### refundCustomer: Missing gateway error handling
- **Location:** L8
- **Issue:** `gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)` has no error handling.
- **Risk:** Runtime crashes, unhandled exceptions.
- **Fix:** Wrap in try-catch, return error state on failure.

### General: Missing return values on failure
- **Location:** L2, L4, L8, L9
- **Issue:** Both functions return `{ ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }` only for customer validation; other failures return nothing (undefined).
- **Risk:** Callers cannot detect when operations fail due to reasons other than missing customer.
- **Fix:** Ensure all error paths return `{ ok: false, error: <message> }`.

## Residual Risks

### Insufficient type validation on gateway parameter
- No validation that the `gateway` parameter exists and has required methods (charge, refund).

### No customer existence verification for refunds
- refundCustomer does not check if refund is valid for the given customer type (some customers may be ineligible for refunds).

### No refund limits or policy checks
- Refund amounts could exceed original charge amounts, which may violate payment gateway policies.

# Code Review Diagnosis

## File: bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs

## Findings

### chargeCustomer: Missing amount validation
- **Location:** L1-L4
- **Issue:** The `amount` parameter is never validated. Type checking, range validation, and positive value checks are missing.
- **Risk:** Invalid or negative amounts can be charged, leading to security and financial issues.
- **Fix:** Add amount type and value validation before calling gateway.charge()

### chargeCustomer: Missing gateway error handling
- **Location:** L3
- **Issue:** `gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)` has no error handling. If the gateway throws or fails, the function will unhandledly crash.
- **Risk:** Runtime crashes, unhandled exceptions, poor developer experience. Function should return proper error state on failure.
- **Fix:** Wrap in try-catch, return `{ ok: false, error: <error-from-gateway> }` on failure

### refundCustomer: Missing customer validation
- **Location:** L7-L9
- **Issue:** No validation that customer exists or has valid ID before attempting refund.
- **Risk:** Refund operation will fail when customer is missing or invalid.
- **Fix:** Check customer.id exists, similar to chargeCustomer.

### refundCustomer: Missing amount validation
- **Location:** L8
- **Issue:** The `amount` parameter is never validated.
- **Risk:** Invalid or negative amounts can be refunded.
- **Fix:** Add amount type and value validation before calling gateway.refund()

### refundCustomer: Missing gateway error handling
- **Location:** L8
- **Issue:** `gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)` has no error handling.
- **Risk:** Runtime crashes, unhandled exceptions.
- **Fix:** Wrap in try-catch, return error state on failure.

### General: Missing return values on failure
- **Location:** L2, L4, L8, L9
- **Issue:** Both functions return `{ ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }` only for customer validation; other failures return nothing (undefined).
- **Risk:** Callers cannot detect when operations fail due to reasons other than missing customer.
- **Fix:** Ensure all error paths return `{ ok: false, error: <message> }`.

## Residual Risks

### Insufficient type validation on gateway parameter
- No validation that the `gateway` parameter exists and has required methods (charge, refund).

### No refund limits or policy checks

**Complete.** File created: `bench-artifacts/code-review-diagnosis.md`

**Findings identified:**
- Missing amount validation in both chargeCustomer and refundCustomer
- Missing gateway error handling in both functions
- Missing customer validation in refundCustomer  
- Missing return values on failure (functions return undefined except for known missing customer error)

**Residual risks:**
- Insufficient type validation on gateway parameter
- No customer existence verification for refunds
- No refund limits or policy checks

**Constraints met:**
- Source file `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs` - not modified
- Exact file path and file included (bench-artifacts/code-review-diagnosis.md)
- Findings-first format with required headings
- No user interaction required to inspect files
